observations.title: Judgment Day approved: consolidation to gentle-ai SDD
observations.content:
**What**: Ran 3-round Judgment Day on consolidation commit 18cb3e4b. Round 1 found 4 confirmed issues (1 CRITICAL: exposed API key in .bak file, 3 WARNING: missing deprecation notices). Round 2 found 1 more missed file. Round 3: both judges returned CLEAN.

**Why**: Needed adversarial verification before merging the consolidation that removed old agent systems.

**Where**: .opencode/opencode.jsonc.bak (deleted), docs/superclaude-codigos/ (3 files got DEPRECATED notices), docs/superpowers/ (4 files got SUPERSEDED notices)

**Learned**: 
- When adding deprecation notices, grep the ENTIRE directory not just files you know about — missed superclaude-comandos-desarrollo.md in first pass
- Judgment Day is effective: Round 1 found a CRITICAL security issue (API key in plaintext) that would have b

observations.title: Completed portfolio redesign with WOW factor
observations.content:
**What**: Rewrote all page templates (index.astro, [slug].astro, news/index.astro) to use the design system CSS variables instead of hardcoded zinc-* Tailwind classes. Replaced all emoji icons with inline SVGs. Added WOW-factor hero with gradient mesh background, grid pattern overlay, status badge, and scroll indicator.

**Why**: User said the site "se ve demasiado básica" — needed to level up the visual design to match modern dark mode UI patterns (Linear/Vercel style).

**Where**: portfolio/src/pages/index.astro, portfolio/src/pages/projects/[slug].astro, portfolio/src/pages/news/index.astro, portfolio/public/favicon.svg

**Learned**: 
- Using inline `style` with CSS vars + `onmouseover`/`onmouseout` for hover states avoids Tailwind class proliferation with CSS variables
- Glass-card cla

observations.title: Implemented Astro native i18n ES/EN with URL routing
observations.content:
**What**: Added full ES/EN internationalization to the Astro 6 portfolio using URL-based routing (no JS toggle). Spanish at `/`, English at `/en/`.

**Why**: SEO-friendly i18n — Google indexes both languages independently. Client-side toggle was rejected.

**Where**:
- `src/i18n/ui.ts` — all translation strings (ES + EN)
- `src/i18n/utils.ts` — `getLang(pathname)`, `useTranslations(lang)`, `getAlternatePath()`, `basePath()`
- `src/components/LangSwitch.astro` — ES/EN pill toggle in nav (desktop + mobile)
- `src/layouts/Base.astro` — detects lang from URL, adds `<html lang>`, hreflang SEO tags, LangSwitch
- `src/content.config.ts` — added `lang: z.enum(['es','en']).default('es')` and `canonicalSlug` to blog schema
- `src/pages/index.astro` — uses `useTranslations('es')`
- `src/pages/blog/in

observations.title: New skills: debug-prod and examya-test
observations.content:
**What**: Created two new Claude Code skills for Examya.

**Why**: Repeated production debugging sessions with manual log sharing — needed a repeatable protocol.

**Where**: 
- `~/.claude/skills/debug-prod/SKILL.md` — production WhatsApp bot debugging via Railway CLI
- `~/.claude/skills/examya-test/SKILL.md` — test creation guide for both workspaces

**Learned**:
- debug-prod skill: service routing (examya-agents for bot behavior, examya-api for orders/payments), Railway commands (`railway logs -n 400 --service examya-agents`), known failure patterns with root causes
- examya-test skill covers both workspaces: agents (Vitest, pipeline pattern, FSM injection) and api (NestJS TestingModule, supertest, Prisma mockDeep, domain tests)
- Railway CLI: `railway status` shows current linked service

observations.title: Sistema Engram bidireccional Mac ↔ VPS activado
observations.content:
**What**: Sistema de memoria bidireccional Engram completamente operativo entre Mac (Claude Code/Cowork) y VPS (Cotocha).

**Why**: Crear un segundo cerebro unificado (estilo Karpathy) donde el trabajo real alimenta automáticamente Obsidian y el blog.

**Where**: 
- Mac: ~/.engram/engram.db, ~/scripts/engram-sync.sh, obsidian-vault/.engram/chunks/
- VPS: ~/.engram/engram.db (134 obs importadas), blog-writer skill actualizado
- GitHub: marioSoftmedic/obsidian-vault (canal de sync via chunks .gz)
- GitHub: marioSoftmedic/marioLanding/context/memory.json (para Cotocha blog-writer)

**Learned**: 
- engram sync --all (no sin flag) para exportar todos los proyectos desde el vault git
- Canal de sync = obsidian-vault repo en GitHub, chunks en .engram/chunks/*.jsonl.gz sin conflictos
- Cotocha bus

**Pipeline**:
1. Meta Cloud API → POST /whatsapp/webhook → handleMetaMediaMessage
2. Quota check (3 free/month per phone)
3. Upload to Supabase Storage bucket `medical-or

observations.title: Created E2E WhatsApp order flow integration tests
observations.content:
**What**: Created comprehensive integration tests that simulate complete multi-step WhatsApp conversations for the medical order flow. All 13 tests pass, no regressions.

**Why**: Previous test coverage only tested isolated fragments. No test verified the full chain: photo → OCR → pendingOcr capture → RUT/email messages → order creation → payment link → post-payment delivery.

**Where**: `apps/api/src/modules/whatsapp/controllers/__tests__/whatsapp-order-flow.spec.ts`

**Learned**:
- Stateful in-memory contextStore: `Map<string, string>` simulates `prismaAgents.whatsappContext`. savePendingOcr writes to it, getPendingOcr reads from it, clearPendingOcr deletes the key — all within a single test.
- Stateful mutable user: `Object.assign(user, data)` in `prisma.user.update` mock allows `update

observations.title: Examya Post-Payment Flow Architecture
observations.content:
**What**: Complete mapping of the WhatsApp medical order quotation system post-payment flow (after PAID status)

**Why**: Understanding the full pipeline of PDF generation, email/WhatsApp delivery, and FONASA pricing computation is critical for feature development, testing, and maintenance

**Where**: 
- Core pipeline: apps/api/src/modules/medical-order-sales/
  - Processors: order-sales.processor.ts (Bull job handler)
  - Services: order-sales-processing.service.ts, order-ready-notification.service.ts, medical-email.service.ts, medical-order-pdf.service.ts, fonasa-pricing.service.ts
  - Utilities: utils/pdf-data.mapper.ts (FONASA level computation, Magallanes handling)

**Learned**: 
1. Post-PAID pipeline is triggered by Bull job 'process-payment-confirmation' (line 275 in order-sales.pro

observations.title: Flow separation analysis: OCR Quotation vs Shuri Purchase — hardening applied
observations.content:
## What
Complete analysis and hardening of the OCR Quotation vs Shuri Purchase flow separation in the WhatsApp bot.

## Why
User asked to verify both flows don't overlap at intent detection, pricing, or PDF generation. Discovered a state contamination risk in PurchaseHandler and a pre-existing coupon discount bug.

## Flow Separation (confirmed independent)

**OCR Quotation flow** (IMAGE message):
- Entry: `handleImageMessage()` → `handleOcrExtraction()` in `whatsapp-webhook.controller.ts`
- Price: `$2,000` from `WHATSAPP_QUOTE_PRICE` env var (read in controller, NOT in agents)
- Creates order with `isQuotation: true`
- PDF: `mapSaleOrder(..., isQuotation=true)` → title "COTIZACIÓN DE EXÁMENES", FONASA pricing (Nacional 3 levels / Magallanes level 3), no doctor section
- pendingOcr pattern

observations.title: Arquitectura sync bidireccional Engram Mac ↔ VPS Cotocha
observations.content:
**What**: Sistema de sync bidireccional de memorias entre Engram local (Mac) y Cotocha (VPS) vía git repo obsidian-vault como transporte.

**Flujo Mac → VPS**: engram-sync.sh --sync-only → exporta chunk → git push obsidian-vault main → VPS cron cada 2h hace git pull + engram sync --import → bridge-engram-to-qmd.py escribe .md en workspace/memory/mac/ → QMD indexa en 5min.

**Flujo VPS → Mac**: bridge-openclaw-engram.py (Option C: parsea session JSONL de /root/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/ + .md files de otros agents) → genera chunk formato {sessions, observations, prompts} como un solo JSON dentro del .jsonl.gz (no JSONL) → git push origin main → engram-sync.sh en Mac absorbe automáticamente a las 7:30am.

**Crons VPS**: bridge export cada 4h (xx:30 UTC), engram import cada 2h (xx:00 UTC

observations.title: ExamIA: STRICT/FLUID composition pattern for medical agent handlers
observations.content:
**What**: Handlers classified as STRICT (template-only) or FLUID (LLM+fallback) based on safety requirements.

STRICT handlers (financial/compliance paths): purchase.handler.ts, cart.handler.ts — do NOT extend BaseHandler, zero LLM calls. Required for CENS compliance.

FLUID handlers (conversational paths): profile.handler.ts, symptom.handler.ts, scheduling.handler.ts — extend BaseHandler, use interpretUserMessage()/generateNaturalReply() with template fallback when LLM returns null or confidence < 0.3.

**Why**: Medical order creation, payment, coupon validation must never include LLM-hallucinated text. Validated by two independent code review agents.

**Where**: apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/handlers/

**Learned**: When nextStage === 'START', handleFsmFlow() returns purchase: {} — stage f

observations.title: Created skill registry in .atl/skill-registry.md
observations.content:

**Learned**: 
- 7 of 8 repo skills are duplicated in .claude/skills/ (canonical is .opencode/skill/)
- development-methodologies is the only skill in .agents/skills/ (workspace-level, no compatibility specified)
- prompt-engineering-patterns is the only skill in .agent/skills/ (agent-level)
- docs/SKILL.md is an empty placeholder
- Registry includes: compact rules per skill, trigger table for orchestrator matching, d

observations.title: Prospects table created - medical centers/clinics discovery
observations.content:
**What**: Created `prospects` table in db_monitor for centros médicos, clínicas privadas, and médicos particulares. Scraped Superdesalud (accredited providers) and Doctoralia.
**Why**: Expand lead generation beyond laboratories to all healthcare providers that could use Examya (ficha clínica, agendamiento, OCR órdenes)
**Where**: db_monitor PostgreSQL, table `prospects` with columns: id, name, name_normalized, segment, type, phone, whatsapp, whatsapp_source, website, region, address, specialty, fonasa_level, rut, registration_number
**Learned**: Superdesalud region pages have data in raw HTML (grep for Centro Médico/Clínica patterns) but are JS-rendered for readability extractor. Article IDs: 8531=Metropolitana, 8532=Valparaíso, 8533=Arica, 8534=Tarapacá, 8535-8543=other regions. Brave Sea

observations.title: Bridge script parses session JSONL directly
observations.content:
**What**: Bridge OpenClaw→Engram ahora es un script Python standalone que parsea session JSONL files directamente, sin depender de mem tools ni agente aislado.
**Why**: Los mem_* tools solo están disponibles dentro de sesiones activas del gateway; no se pueden llamar desde scripts externos. El gateway WS requiere auth propietario que no pudimos replicar.
**Where**: /root/clawd/skills/self-improve/scripts/bridge-openclaw-engram.py
**Learned**: Las observaciones de mem_save se guardan como toolCall blocks en los session JSONL files con formato {type:'toolCall', name:'mem_save', arguments:{title,content,type,project,...}}. El script lee todos los JSONL, extrae mem_save calls, deduplica por title+project, y genera chunks Engram válidos. Cron usa agente aislado con GLM-4.7-flash para ejecuta

observations.title: Phone priority in PurchaseHandler: patient_phone > phone > from
observations.content:
**What**: La prioridad del campo `patientPhone` en handlePayment() es `patient_phone ?? phone ?? from` (en ese orden).
**Why**: Judgment Day sugirió invertirlo a `from` primero por ser el campo canónico de WhatsApp, pero los tests PHONE-01/PHONE-02 documentan que `patient_phone` (número registrado del paciente) tiene prioridad sobre `from` (número fuente WA). El Judgment Day estaba equivocado.
**Where**: apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/handlers/purchase.handler.ts — handlePayment(), orderPayload.patientPhone
**Learned**: NUNCA cambiar prioridad de campos sin revisar tests existentes primero. Los tests PHONE-01/PHONE-02/PHONE-03 en purchase.handler.spec.ts documentan este contrato explícitamente.

observations.title: Shuri Agent Architectural Hardening (CENS Compliance)
observations.content:
**What**: Restructured ShuriAgent to use a clean orchestration pipeline with a top-level urgency guard and class-based memory.
**Why**: Architectural stabilization for CENS compliance (ISO 25010) and fixing persistent test regressions in flow transitions.
**Where**: apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/shuri-agent.ts, apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/shuri-memory.ts, apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/domain/shuri-utils.ts
**Learned**: Prioritizing safety guards (Urgency) at the start of `processMessage` ensures compliance without breaking complex FSM stage logic. Terminal stages must clear local state objects immediately to satisfy state-dependent tests.

observations.title: Judgment Day - Shuri Agent Compliance Verdict
observations.content:
**What**: Synthesized the final Judgment Day verdict for the Shuri Agent hardening.
**Why**: Adversarial review confirmed critical architectural improvements for CENS compliance.
**Where**: apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/shuri-agent.ts, apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/handlers/purchase.handler.ts, apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/services/medical-summary.service.ts
**Learned**: The system is now resilient against RUT brute-force (3-retry limit) and payment duplication (idempotency guard). A clinical summary is now guaranteed upon order completion. One minor suggestion (RUT normalization) was identified.

observations.title: Fixed Shuri WhatsApp bot conversation flow bugs
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed 3 bugs + 1 persistence issue in Shuri WhatsApp bot: (1) routing precedence so purchase stage handlers run before package_recommendation, (2) intent router now receives stage context and uses it in cache key + deterministic confirmation bypass, (3) Laboratorio prompt now mentions symptom-to-exam suggestion, (4) campaign flow persistence path mismatch fixed.

**Why**: User reported bot losing context between messages, "si, generala" being misrouted to package suggestions instead of generating medical order, and missing symptom suggestion UX.

**Where**: 
- `apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/shuri-agent.ts` — confirmation guard before isPackageRecommendation, userChangedIntent protection, symptom mention in prompts
- `apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/intent-router.ts` — RouteContext pa

observations.title: Fixed symptom-to-order quote: uses FONASA codes over display names
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed symptom-to-order quote flow: `quoteMedicalOrder` was `shuri-agent.ts` now uses `suggestedExamCodes` (FONASA codes like "0301045") as primary search input instead of display names which were truncated to 40 chars and The search API (`/medical-order-sales/exams/search`) receives code-only FONASA codes query, works correctly. In `quote_medical_order.cjs`, when codes are found, it resolves and API item with correct `examCode`.

**Why**: User reported "Si, generala" still shows "cotización failed" instead of generating order. Evidence from logs shows API search works with codes queries ( but display name query fails because names are truncated to 40 chars).
**Where**: `apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/shuri-agent.ts` lines 2213-2217, `apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/handlers/quote_medi
